objects.name: Process Doppelganging
objects.description: Process Doppelganging is an evasion technique where malware creates a new transaction, writes its payload to a file, creates a section backed by the file, and then aborts the transaction so the file never appears on disk, allowing execution of malicious code without leaving traces.

objects.name: DLL Search-Order Hijacking
objects.description: DLL search-order hijacking is a technique where malware places a malicious DLL in a location that is searched before the legitimate DLL, causing the application to load the malicious DLL instead.

objects.name: Heaven’s Gate
objects.description: Heaven's Gate is a technique used by malware to switch between 32-bit and 64-bit code execution, often to evade detection and analysis tools.

objects.name: API Hashing
objects.description: API hashing is an evasion technique where malware resolves API addresses dynamically using hash values instead of clear-text names, making analysis and detection more difficult.

objects.name: Shellcode Injection into System DLLs
objects.description: Malware injects shellcode into the .text section of legitimate system DLLs (such as pla.dll, vssapi.dll, d3d9.dll) and executes code from those libraries to evade detection.

objects.name: Social Engineering via Malicious SVG
objects.description: Attackers use a malicious SVG file containing embedded JavaScript that drops a password-protected archive and displays the password to the user, prompting them to extract and execute the contained malware.

objects.name: Persistence via Startup Folder Shortcut
objects.description: Malware achieves persistence by dropping a .lnk shortcut in the user's Startup folder, pointing to a loader executable that initiates the infection chain.

objects.name: Antimalware Process Monitoring
objects.description: Malware monitors running processes by enumerating them and comparing their names (via hash or checksum) against a blocklist of antimalware products, altering execution if a match is found.

objects.name: Anti-Hooking
objects.description: Malware loads a clean copy of ntdll.dll, compares exported function bytes to the in-memory copy, and restores original bytes to bypass userland hooks and debugger breakpoints.

objects.name: dragon_util.dll
objects.description: Malicious DLL used for side-loading and code injection. Tampered version with invalid certificate.

objects.name: SVG File
objects.description: Malicious SVG file containing embedded JavaScript that drops a password-protected archive.

objects.name: Password Protected Archive File
objects.description: Archive dropped by malicious SVG. Contains HijackLoader payload. Password: 89G4UYYT.

objects.name: dragon_helper.exe
objects.description: Benign executable used for DLL side-loading. Signed by Comodo Security Solutions Inc.

objects.name: PDFPreviewHandlerHost.exe (TController128.exe)
objects.description: Benign executable renamed by malware for code injection and C2 communication. Signed by FOXIT SOFTWARE INC.

objects.name: Chime.exe
objects.description: Benign executable used for code injection. Signed by Amazon.com Services LLC.

objects.name: Eet.pt
objects.description: Encoded shellcode blob used in the infection chain.

objects.name: Mearsheeg.yj
objects.description: Encoded shellcode blob used in the infection chain.

objects.name: Final payload injected in TController128.exe
objects.description: Payload injected into TController128.exe using process doppelganging and Heaven's Gate techniques.

objects.name: HijackLoader C2 Server
objects.description: Command and control server used by HijackLoader for remote control and data exfiltration.
